title: Freshly Aerated Northern-Thai Purple Sprouting Broccoli Tagine
description: This light and earthy Northern-Thai inspired dish is a unique take on traditional tagine recipes, featuring freshly aerated purple sprouting broccoli, grilled to perfection using a robata grill and served in a ceramic tagine vessel.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a spice grinder, blend coriander seeds and cumin into a fine powder.
In a large bowl, combine the minced galangal, sliced chilies, and spice powder.
Trim the broccoli into florets and add to the bowl, mixing well to coat.
Heat coconut oil in the ceramic tagine over medium-high heat.
Add the broccoli mixture to the tagine and cook for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add water to the tagine, bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 8 minutes.
Season with salt and fish sauce (if using), to taste.
To aerate the broccoli, remove the tagine from the heat and let it sit for 2 minutes.
Return the tagine to medium heat and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring gently to distribute heat evenly.
Serve immediately, garnished with toasted coriander seeds and fresh herbs if desired.
Clean and store the ceramic tagine for future use.
